name: Pico Jano and Cruz de la Viorna Loop
name_original: Pico Jano y Cruz de la Viorna – circular desde La Vega
description: This difficult 16.9-mile mountain biking route from La Vega offers panoramic views of the Picos de Europa from Pico Jano and La Viorna Cross
region_intro: Mountain bike trails in Lic-Rio Deva traverse a diverse natural environment within the Natura 2000 network, encompassing parts of Cantabria and Asturias. The region is characterized by the dramatic La Hermida Gorge, where the Deva River flows between the Ándara Massif foothills and the Peñarrubia mountain range. Mountainous terrain, including areas near the Picos de Europa, offers varied elevations and scenic backdrops for mountain biking. Alluvial forests of alder and ash line the watercourses, contributing to the area's rich biodiversity. Best mountain bike trails in Lic-Rio Deva The most popular mountain bike trail is Ándara Massif Loop, a 41.8 miles (67.3 km) difficult trail that takes about 7 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route explores the rugged terrain of the Ándara Massif foothills. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa – circular por Invernales de Igüedri, Peña Oviedo y Mogrovejo, a difficult 18.4 miles (29.6 km) path. This route offers expansive views within the Picos de Europa National Park. Local mountain bikers also love the Portes – circular por Cabezón de Liébana y Porcieda, a 15.6 miles (25.1 km) trail leading through the Liébana area, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking in Lic-Rio Deva is defined by deep river gorges, mountainous landscapes, and dense forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Lic-Rio Deva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lic-Rio Deva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: There are over 45 mountain bike trails in Lic-Rio Deva, offering a diverse range of options for all skill levels. This includes 1 easy route, 18 moderate routes, and 27 difficult routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Lic-Rio Deva, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. While summer is also popular, some trails can be very warm. Winter riding is possible but may involve colder temperatures and potentially challenging conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Yes, Lic-Rio Deva offers some easier routes suitable for families or beginners. These trails are typically up to 2 hours long with less than 1,000 feet of elevation gain, making them accessible for a more relaxed riding experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Mountain bike trails in Lic-Rio Deva feature diverse terrain, from deep river gorges and mountainous landscapes to dense forests. You'll encounter limestone gorges, alluvial forests along watercourses, and rugged mountain paths, especially in areas bordering the Picos de Europa.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can ride through the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hermida Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, where the Deva River carves its way between mountain ranges. The area is also known for its rich biodiversity, including alluvial forests of alder and ash, and the chance to spot wildlife like otters and kingfishers along the riverbanks.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer expansive views of the Picos de Europa National Park?
A: Yes, several routes provide stunning vistas of the Picos de Europa. One such route is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000628222/parque-nacional-de-picos-de-europa-circular-por-invernales-de-igueedri-pena-oviedo-y-mogrovejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa – circular por Invernales de Igüedri, Peña Oviedo y Mogrovejo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive views within the national park.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Lic-Rio Deva offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169839644/andara-massif-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ándara Massif Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 67.3 km trail exploring rugged terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000641335/espinama-fuente-de-puerto-de-pandetrave-parque-nacional-de-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espinama - Fuente Dé - puerto de Pandetrave – Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: The mountain bike routes in Lic-Rio Deva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the dramatic scenery of the gorges and mountains, and the sense of adventure offered by the trails.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169366964/portes-circular-por-cabezon-de-liebana-y-porcieda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portes – circular por Cabezón de Liébana y Porcieda&lt;/a&gt; is a 25.1 km loop through the Liébana area. Another challenging loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000673119/pico-jano-and-cruz-de-la-viorna-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Jano and Cruz de la Viorna Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/991425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Potes&lt;/a&gt;, a charming town nearby. Other notable attractions include the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Lebeña Church&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4135071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Infantado Tower&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific climbs or ascents known in the region?
A: Yes, the region features notable ascents due to its mountainous terrain. One well-known climb is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7402100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Fuente Dé from Potes&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a significant challenge and rewarding views.
tips: Pico Jano is the highest peak of the Sierra de Collaín, which separates the valleys of Cereceda and Valdebaró. Despite its modest height, it stands as an extraordinary vantage point over the whole of the Lebanese region. The summit offers a 360º panorama full of high mountains that flank the three great valleys of Deva, Quiviesa and Bullón.

La Cruz de la Viorna is located at 1086 meters above sea level and given its large size it can be seen from kilometers away. It can be reached either from Potes or from La Vega and you can combine it with the ascent to Pico Jano.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Pico Jano y Cruz de la Viorna mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route is estimated to take around 3 hours and 35 minutes to complete. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, technical skills, and how often you stop to enjoy the panoramic views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied and demanding. You'll encounter a mix of paths, hiking paths, streets, and state roads, with a substantial portion being unpaved. Expect sections of loose and steep terrain, tough single track, and areas with rocks and loose material. There are also off-camber and bumpy road sections, particularly on descents where a strong smell of burning brakes is anticipated due to the steepness.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers exceptional panoramic views, especially from its two main highlights. You'll pass by Jano Peak, the highest point of the Sierra de Collaín, which provides an extraordinary 360º panorama of the Picos de Europa and the Liébana Valley. Another key landmark is the La Viorna Cross, a prominent cross visible from afar, also offering impressive vistas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The circular route typically starts from La Vega. You should be able to find parking in or near the village to begin your ride. It's always a good idea to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as difficult and is not suitable for beginners. It requires very good physical fitness and advanced technical riding skills. Riders should be prepared for tough, steep, and technical sections, both uphill and downhill, with strong gradients and loose stones.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Pico Jano y Cruz de la Viorna route?
faqs.answer: Given its mountainous location and challenging terrain, the best time to ride this route is generally during the late spring, summer, and early autumn months. This period typically offers more favorable weather conditions, reducing the likelihood of snow, ice, or excessive mud, which could make an already difficult trail even more hazardous.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access the Pico Jano y Cruz de la Viorna mountain bike trail. It is generally open to the public.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Jano y Cruz de la Viorna mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there is no specific information stating whether dogs are allowed on this particular trail, its difficult classification and technical terrain suggest it might not be ideal for all dogs. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is fit enough for a strenuous ride and always keep it on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other trail users.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will find parts of the Transcantábrica trail (covering approximately 22% of the route), the [CIMA CA03] San Glorio - Collada de Llesba (about 13%), and a small portion of the La Vega trail (1%).

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect truly stunning and diverse scenery. The route offers impressive panoramic views of the Picos de Europa and the entire Liébana Valley from high vantage points like Jano Peak and La Viorna Cross. Additionally, you'll pass through pleasant forests of holm oaks and oaks, adding a natural, wooded element to the ride.

faqs.question: Are there any alternative starting points or route variations for this area?
faqs.answer: While the circular route typically starts from La Vega, the La Viorna Cross can also be accessed from Potes. Some versions of trails in this area may include a descent towards the stunning village of Potes or pass through the abandoned village of Porcieda along sections of the Camino Lebaniego, offering different perspectives and experiences.
